title: Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein: Behind the Scenes with the Crew
text: Netflix's Frankenstein adaptation features an acclaimed below-the-line team bringing director Guillermo del Toro's artistic vision to life, with the film garnering nine Oscar nominations including recognition for costume design, production design, and hair/makeup. Production designer Tamara Deverell describes del Toro's approach as driven by a desire for something operatic and theatrical, drawing inspiration from Caravaggio paintings. Costume designer Kate Hawley notes that the Tiffany's archive was opened up for the production's use, a rarity for Hollywood. Color plays a crucial role with red representing Victor's childhood brought through his story, while yellow bands worn by Jacob Elordi's creature carry Christ-like significance. Del Toro envisioned Victor Frankenstein as rock-star-like. The creature design required extraordinary effort with prosthetics designer Mike Hill revealing the process took ten hours daily, requiring five people to apply 54 silicon pieces. Elordi worked on an alternating schedule with midnight call times and a portable sauna needed for removal.
